scenarios.name: Base — the grind
scenarios.thesis: Autonomous substitution continues ~+2pp/yr; the ratio drifts into the mid-20s by end-2026. The US channel stays exhausted, allied displacement begins, and frontier chips keep lagging tools. Controls remain near-irrelevant to the aggregate number.
scenarios.confirming: Ratio holds a 22-28% band; domestic semicap revenue grows YoY; chip share stays <16%.
scenarios.falsifying: A >5pp single-quarter jump (discontinuity), or domestic revenue turning YoY-negative.
scenarios.exposed: Cycle-neutral long domestic winners (Naura/AMEC/ACM) vs short foreign losers (AMAT/LRCX); the base case for the exposure ladder.

scenarios.name: Accelerate — the ceiling lifts
scenarios.thesis: A credible domestic advanced-lithography milestone (SMEE / Huawei-linked SiCarrier) relieves the one un-respinnable chokepoint. Substitution accelerates, foreign vendors lose China faster than the cycle explains, and the ratio pushes >27% — the chip controls start to look moot.
scenarios.confirming: Ratio >27%; domestic revenue YoY >20%; foreign-vendor China-% falling faster than total China WFE demand.
scenarios.falsifying: Litho remains stuck at mature-node DUV; no domestic EUV/immersion progress.
scenarios.exposed: Amplifies the base trade; adds litho-adjacent (Kingsemi de-bottlenecks) and pressures ASML China DUV harder.

scenarios.name: Stall — the chokepoint binds
scenarios.thesis: The lithography chokepoint holds; a capex-cycle downturn hits capital-starved domestic makers harder than incumbents; allied controls tighten and force reliance shifts. The ratio stalls below ~22% and the substitution story pauses.
scenarios.confirming: Ratio flat-to-down; domestic revenue YoY-negative; allied (NL/Japan) controls tighten materially.
scenarios.falsifying: Domestic revenue keeps compounding through a cycle trough.
scenarios.exposed: Unwinds the cycle-neutral trade; foreign incumbents (AMAT/LRCX/KLA) recover China share; the theme goes dormant.
